title: Reusable Workflows, Composite Actions, Interfaces, and Permission Flow · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Reuse Component Selection
summary: Teams copy release YAML across repositories; a shared workflow changes an input silently, accepts all secrets, escalates permissions, and hides which workflow revision produced an artifact. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci reuse component selection increment in versioned reusable automation contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose reusable workflow, composite action, JavaScript action, Docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority.
Use workflow schema checks, policy engines, dependency review, secret scanning, and CodeQL for Actions to detect unsafe patterns and exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

  deploy-cloudrun: 3

steps.title: Predict Ci Reuse Component Selection · Theory Ci Reuse Component Selection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ci Reuse Component Selection contract for the supplied data.

For Predict Ci Reuse Component Selection · Theory Ci Reuse Component Selection, keep Ci Reuse Component Selection connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name event, workflow, revision, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, user-signal, and recovery boundaries; predict every state and trust transition; review the smallest deterministic workflow or release change; then verify a fault, repair, rollback, and authorized live-system transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Ci Reuse Component Selection while producing versioned reusable automation contract.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Choose reusable workflow, composite action, JavaScript action, Docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority. A defensible solution applies Ci Reuse Component Selection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned reusable automation contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-reuse-component-selection — Choose reusable workflow, composite action, JavaScript action, Docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-2
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-2
    artifact: reusable-automation.yaml
    decision: worked gha reuse composite workflow contracts 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      component: reusable-workflow
      workflow-call: typed-inputs-secrets-outputs
      permissions: caller-bounded
      secret-inheritance: explicit-only
      environment-secret: deployment-job-only
      external-ref: full-workflow-sha
      producer-proof: workflow-ref-and-sha
      compatibility: consumer-contract-suite
      deprecation: measured-migration
    verify: validate caller and callee interfaces permissions secrets outputs pins nested depth changed consumer cases and rollback
    failure: Reject wrapper equivalence, implicit secret inheritance, permission escalation, default-branch refs, or centralization as drift elimination.
    repair: Choose the correct boundary, type the interface, preserve caller limits, pin identity, test consumers, canary change, and deprecate visibly.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Reusable workflows and composite actions are interchangeable wrappers. This fails the Ci Reuse Component Selection contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-reuse-component-selection-1swvbh
  - competency: ci-reuse-component-selection
    artifact: reusable-automation.yaml
    decision: reuse component selection
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: restore a cache written from a lower-trust branch
    retained-proof: OIDC claims provider condition service account IAM and audit entry
    case-key: 1swvbh
    change: |
      component: reusable-workflow
      workflow-call: typed-inputs-secrets-outputs
      permissions: caller-bounded
      secret-inheritance: explicit-only
      environment-secret: deployment-job-only
      external-ref: full-workflow-sha
      producer-proof: workflow-ref-and-sha
      compatibility: consumer-contract-suite
      deprecation: measured-migration
    verify: validate caller and callee interfaces permissions secrets outputs pins nested depth changed consumer cases and rollback
    failure: Reject wrapper equivalence, implicit secret inheritance, permission escalation, default-branch refs, or centralization as drift elimination.
    repair: Choose the correct boundary, type the interface, preserve caller limits, pin identity, test consumers, canary change, and deprecate visibly.
